description: Manually defined QE/risk-on windows from Fed policy announcements, M2 money supply growth (FRED M2SL), and SPY price history. NOT from Ilmanen (2003) or Campbell et al (2014) — Ilmanen identifies stock-bond decorrelation episodes ending 2001; Campbell et al identifies a structural regime 2001Q1-2011Q4 (entire decade, different granularity). These windows are from FOMC announcement dates, FRED M2 data, and financial news.
dateSource: FOMC statements, FRED M2SL series, SPY/TLT price history via Yahoo Finance.

eventWindows.label: Post-GFC QE1 risk-on: M2 growth 6-9% YoY, SPY momentum confirmed
eventWindows.note: Window chosen from FRED M2SL showing 6-9% YoY growth and SPY recovering above 63-day SMA. Ends when M2 YoY decelerates below 4%.

eventWindows.label: 2019 Fed pivot: 3 rate cuts Jul-Oct 2019, repo facility expansion
Post-COVID stimulus: M2 growth 14-28% YoY (FRED M2SL), unprecedented monetary expansion
eventWindows.note: M2 YoY growth stayed above 10% through mid-2021. Window from FRED data, not from any paper.

passCriterion: Detector fires within 10 trading days of window start. No coverage requirement — QE signal is episodic.
